title: Fix SystemExec completion race
body:
Some callers assume when SystemExec returns, the command has completed.
Other callers explicitly wait for completion (as required).  However,
forgetting to do that is an incredibly easy mistake to make.  Fix this
by adding an explicit parameter to the function.  This requires
every caller to deliberately state whether or not a completion-check
is required.

Also address **many** resource naming / cleanup completion-races.

Signed-off-by: Chris Evich <cevich@redhat.com>

comments.body: @cevich Just FYI I think skip-ci breaks the mergebot

@baude @mheon this is what I'm thinkin'   and means I'll need to seek out and find every caller and figure out if it was intended to wait or not.   Though in the grand-scheme, this is probably also a good thing.  Opinions?

@cevich I would initially default them all to true and see what breaks, IMO

No problems with the approach though

(some people and their cats have WAY too much free time)

If you want to do things the hard way , have a separate interface.
SystemExecInBackground()
Most likely no one will use, but we can have it for the potential use.
Oh this is so much nicer! Thank you! Reviewing, first pass looks good, but I need another pass and it will have to be later today. I will do my best to finish this morning.
Yeah it is a lot better, thanks for the suggestion and the reviews.  Hrmmm...thought I fixed that podman pull goof (failing tests)...
ok, think I found problem.  Run 'em again...
@baude @mheon PTAL this fixes that ``podman run n user created network namespace with resolv.conf`` flake and quite a few other potential resource/name conflicts.
